original_filename: Staff Awaiting DBS Risk Assessment V2.0FEB24.docx

questions.question_text: Did the applicant submit a fully completed application form, including all previous employment and gaps in employment?
Did the applicant fully complete and sign the practice application form?

Did the applicant declare any criminal convictions, bindovers, cautions, reprimands, or police investigations that might lead to a conviction, orders binding them over, or cautions in the UK or any other country?
If yes, are these convictions relevant to the work that they are being employed to undertake?
If yes, are these convictions of a serious nature, i.e. offences against children / vulnerable adults / violent or sexual offences?
If yes, please state details
Did the applicant declare they had been subject to any disciplinary investigation or action, including suspension?
Have you obtained references?
Do the individual's references give any cause for concern?
At the interview, did the individual say or do anything that gave cause for concern in relation to allowing them to commence work before a disclosure is received?
Has the individual ever had a DBS/CRB/police check previously?
If yes, when was this done and with whom (employer)?
Does the job involve regularly caring for, training, supervising, or being in sole charge of children?
Will the individual be required to work alone during their induction period?
Will the individual have access to or opportunity to commit an offence against a patient?
If yes, what measures will you put in place to prevent this from happening?

Are you satisfied that the risk of possible offending can be minimised by ensuring that satisfactory supervisory measures listed above can be implemented to prevent the individual from being alone with members of the public
Please outline the implications of delaying the individual in commencing employment:
I have considered the questions outlined above, and I am not satisfied that it is safe to allow the above-named individual to commence work before the Disclosure clearance is received.

I have considered the questions outlined above and confirm that I am satisfied that it is safe to allow the above-named individual to commence work before the Disclosure clearance is received, subject to the safety measures detailed above being in place.

title: Staff Awaiting DBS Risk Assessment V2.0FEB24
